capability_objective: The duty manager needs two customer cases reconciled for shift handoff. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the case named “Accessibility Caption Delay” at “Elm Learning” and the case named “Incorrect Renewal Date” at “Frost Dental”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use exactly three bullets: one per record in the requested order, then one status-comparison bullet; include both stable IDs.
prompt:
The duty manager needs two customer cases reconciled for shift handoff. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read SUPPORT_CASES.md and use Pi's shell tool to run the genuine local ./bin/casectl executable; use its built-in help to discover the interface. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, backing database, runtime evidence, signing material, reference setup, or protected verifier. Do not write a script or wrapper to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the case named “Accessibility Caption Delay” at “Elm Learning” and the case named “Incorrect Renewal Date” at “Frost Dental”. Your first case-data assistant action must launch both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct ./bin/casectl processes, with no other case operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both searches have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following case-data assistant action must launch both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct ./bin/casectl processes. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take status values only from successful full-record gets.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not access the backing store directly. Do not expose unrelated matches or extra full-record fields.

Write handoff_report.md with exactly three nonblank Markdown bullet lines and no heading or other content. Use these exact shapes in the requested order, replacing placeholders:
- Accessibility Caption Delay at Elm Learning — ID: <stable-ID-or-unresolved>; status: <retrieved-status-or-unresolved>
- Incorrect Renewal Date at Frost Dental — ID: <stable-ID-or-unresolved>; status: <retrieved-status-or-unresolved>
- Status comparison: <comparison>

For a resolved branch, preserve the returned ID and status spelling exactly. For an unresolved branch, use the literal lowercase word unresolved for both its ID and status. If both statuses resolve and differ, the comparison text must be “Accessibility Caption Delay is <first-status>, while Incorrect Renewal Date is <second-status>; the statuses differ.” If both resolve and match, use “both cases have status <status>.” Otherwise state which case or cases are unresolved and that the statuses cannot be compared. After writing the report, run python3 -B .protected/verify.py. Leave all supplied and protected files unchanged. Return only the same three bullets in your final response.
